summary: Matched a large portion of the Taru item implementation by replacing many placeholder stubs in `src/melee/it/items/ittaru.c` with real item spawn, physics, collision, animation, damage, and explosion/state-transition logic. The PR also added a Taru special-attributes struct to `itCommonItems.h`, corrected Taru item-var layout so `xDE8` is a `Vec3`, gave `it_802886C4` a typed prototype, and noted a shared float register-swap issue also present in `ittarucann.c`. Evidence is mostly from the diff; the PR body only says "more claude work" and there was no visible review feedback.
what_changed: Added `itTaruAttributes` in `itCommonItems.h` with two integer fields followed by float tuning fields through offset `x34`, enabling typed access to Taru article special attributes.

Updated `itTaru_ItemVars` comments and layout, changing the final item-var member at `ip+DE8` from a scalar `f32 xDE8` to `Vec3 xDE8`; the matched code uses this as a surface/up vector with `.x/.y/.z`.

Implemented `it_3F14_Logic2_Spawned`, initializing facing direction, item command variables, Taru item vars, randomized base rotation using `HSD_Randf`, and default `xDE8` vector before entering `it_80287D0C`.

Implemented helper movement/rotation routines `it_802874F0` and `it_80287690`, including ground-vs-air angular velocity behavior, speed clamping from Taru attributes, facing flips, child JObj rotation easing, and parent JObj Z rotation updates.

Implemented several Taru motion animation and collision callbacks, including `itTaru_UnkMotion0_Coll`, `itTaru_UnkMotion1_Coll`, `itTaru_UnkMotion3_Anim/Coll`, `itTaru_UnkMotion4_Anim/Phys`, and `itTaru_UnkMotion5_Anim/Coll`.

Implemented landing/bounce/rolling behaviors using item collision helpers such as `it_8026D564`, `it_8026DA08`, `it_802763E0`, `it_80276308`, `it_80276408`, `it_8027649C`, `it_802762B0`, and `it_802762BC`.

Implemented `it_802886C4` as a typed `void it_802886C4(Item_GObj* gobj)` routine that spawns/handles Taru cannon behavior: hit/camera effects, spawn check helper, hiding the JObj, zeroing velocity, setting flags/item vars, and switching animation state 7.

Implemented Taru hit-response callbacks `it_3F14_Logic2_DmgDealt`, `Clanked`, `HitShield`, `Reflected`, and `DmgReceived`, sharing an inline helper that spawns effect `0x427` and randomly chooses between `it_802886C4` and `it_802885C8`.
Updated `ittaru.h` prototype for `it_802886C4` from `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` to a concrete `void`/`Item_GObj*` signature.
Added a comment in `ittarucann.c` documenting that the spawn-time float register-swap issue is the same as in `it_3F14_Logic2_Spawned`.
smart_moves: Introduced `itTaruAttributes` rather than continuing to index opaque `x4_specialAttributes`, making physics thresholds, probabilities, fall/bounce limits, and speed clamps clearer and easier to match.
Recognized `itTaru_ItemVars.xDE8` as a `Vec3`, which aligns with calls like `it_80276408` and `lbVector_AngleXY` and avoids treating vector state as unrelated scalar fields.

Used small static inline helpers for repeated logic: `inline_fabsf`, `itTaru_RandCheck`, `it_3F14_Logic2_inline`, `itTaru_UnkMotion4_Phys_inline`, and `it_802886C4_inline`. This reduced duplicated hit/explosion code while still preserving matching-sensitive code shape.
Preserved matching-sensitive awkwardness explicitly with comments such as the float register-swap TODO and manual absolute-value expressions where normal `fabsf` caused register swaps.
Shared identical behavior among damage-dealt, clanked, hit-shield, and reflected callbacks via one inline helper while retaining the stack padding needed for matching (`PAD_STACK(24)`).
Kept motion callbacks returning `false` where appropriate, matching item callback conventions even when the routine performs state transitions internally.

Used typed engine helpers and macros (`GET_ITEM`, `GET_JOBJ`, `HSD_JObjGetChild`, `HSD_JObjSetRotationZ/X`, `HSD_Randf`, `HSD_Randi`) instead of raw pointer arithmetic where the types were understood.
decomp_lessons: When an item-var block is used as a vector by collision and vector math helpers, update the struct layout to a `Vec3`; correct struct typing can unlock multiple matches in motion/collision code.
For item special attributes, adding a dedicated attributes struct with offset comments is preferable to repeated casts and magic offsets, even if field names remain offset-based.
Matching item physics often requires preserving manual absolute-value code instead of replacing it with `fabsf`, because libcall/intrinsic choice and register allocation can perturb matching.
Repeated item hit callbacks can often be matched with static inline shared bodies, but stack padding may still be necessary to keep each wrapper matching.

Surface interaction code commonly combines `CollData.env_flags`, `it_80276408` normal extraction, `lbVector_AngleXY`, and JObj rotation correction; this pattern is reusable for rolling or tilting ground items.
Spawn/setup routines should initialize item command variables, facing direction, custom item vars, and any derived orientation vector before entering the initial motion/state helper.
For damage/explosion item logic, guard on an item-var state flag before triggering one-shot transitions so repeated collision/hit callbacks do not retrigger effects.
naming_conventions: Kept unknown Taru callbacks in the existing address/logic style: `it_3F14_Logic2_*` and `itTaru_UnkMotionN_{Anim,Phys,Coll}`.

Named the newly typed article attributes struct `itTaruAttributes`, matching project item-attribute naming conventions such as `itNokoNoko_DatAttrs` nearby while using offset-based field names (`x0`, `x4`, `x8`, etc.) because semantic names are not yet established.
Retained offset-based custom item-var fields in `itTaru_ItemVars` (`xDD4`, `xDD8`, `xDDC`, `xDE0`, `xDE4`, `xDE8`) and added comments tying them to `ip+DD4` style offsets.
Updated function prototype style from unknown placeholders to concrete project types once known: `void it_802886C4(Item_GObj* gobj)`.
Inline helper names were descriptive only where semantics were apparent (`itTaru_RandCheck`), otherwise they preserved address/context names (`it_3F14_Logic2_inline`, `it_802886C4_inline`).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(24)` in several hit-response callbacks, indicating stack layout needed explicit padding for matching.
Documented a float register-swap issue in `it_3F14_Logic2_Spawned` and cross-referenced the same issue in `it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned` in `ittarucann.c`.
Avoided standard `fabsf` in key places and used manual sign checks or a local `inline_fabsf` because alternate fabs implementations caused register swaps.
Used static inline helpers to control code generation for shared repeated sequences while keeping wrappers small and matchable.
Kept unusual temporaries such as `new_var`, `new_var2`, `var_f0`, `var_f1`, and `var_f2`, suggesting register-allocation-sensitive matching rather than cleanup-oriented refactoring.

Used explicit float constants in radians (`1.5707964f`, `0.34906584f`, `1.3089969f`, `0.12217305f`, `0.10471976f`, `0.017453292f`) instead of symbolic conversions, likely to match original constants exactly.
Inserted an explicit zero-vector local in `it_802886C4_inline` and noted a `@todo 4 byte gap for these accesses`, preserving stack/access behavior despite unclear structure.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body only states "more claude work", so there is no direct human explanation of intent beyond the title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename `itTaruAttributes` fields from offset names to semantic names.
Search for follow-up fixes to the documented float register-swap TODO in `it_3F14_Logic2_Spawned` or `it_3F14_Logic5_Spawned`.
Check whether `it_802886C4_inline`'s noted 4-byte stack/access gap was later resolved with a more accurate local/struct layout.
Compare Taru and Tarucann spawn/motion code for shared patterns that could inform future item matching.
Search for later reviews or commits involving `itTaru_ItemVars.xDE8` to confirm the `Vec3` interpretation remained stable.
